pages.text: Miri likes to crunch her toast.
How do you eat, flamingo? I eat upside down! I trap shrimp in my beak.
How do you eat, dung beetle? I roll dung into a ball. I chew the wet bits and my babies munch the grass.
How do you eat, pangolin? I lick up ants with my long, sticky tongue.
How do you eat, earthworm? I don’t have teeth. I swallow soil!
How do you eat, tickbird? I ride on animals and snack on their ticks and earwax.
How do you eat, whale? I sing to find my supper. The sound bounces off the fish!
How do you eat, tortoise? I munch prickly pears with my hard mouth.
How do you eat, secretary bird? I stomp on snakes. I slurp them up!
How do you eat, giraffe? I stretch my neck and grab the highest leaves.
How do you eat, ostrich? I swallow little stones to break up my food.
Miri, what is the best way to eat? Oh, she’s too busy to chat! M u n c h! G! o w b b e l h e C! S! e l l b u b r i p N BURP!! N i b! p Excuse me! b r u l e l S! C! w h e e h w C!! e N l b b i
